description:
You are a support-engineer bot.  
    Your customer has already uploaded two application log files to the Linux
    box you are connected to:

        /home/user/support/logs/app.log
        /home/user/support/logs/app2.log

    1. Create a new directory called
           /home/user/support/diagnostics
       (create all parent directories only if they do not already exist).

    2. Extract every line that contains the literal word “ERROR” (case-sensitive)
       from both source logs **in the order the files are listed above** and write
       them, one per line, into a text file named  

           /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors_combined.log

       • Keep each line exactly as it appears in the original logs  
         (no leading/trailing whitespace may be added or removed).  
       • Preserve the original line-ordering within each file and
         keep the file order: all “ERROR” lines from *app.log* first,
         followed by all “ERROR” lines from *app2.log*.

    3. Produce a four-line summary file named  

           /home/user/support/diagnostics/diagnostic_report.txt

       with *precisely* these four lines (nothing more, nothing less):

           Line-1: “app.log total lines: <N1>”
           Line-2: “app2.log total lines: <N2>”
           Line-3: “total ERROR lines: <N3>”
           Line-4: “first ERROR line: <exact text of the first ERROR line in errors_combined.log>”

       where  
           • <N1> is the total number of lines present in /home/user/support/logs/app.log  
           • <N2> is the total number of lines present in /home/user/support/logs/app2.log  
           • <N3> is the number of lines in errors_combined.log  
           • The “first ERROR line” must be copied verbatim (no extra/removed
             whitespace) from the first line of errors_combined.log.

       The automated tests will read both newly created files and must find:

           – Exactly four lines in diagnostic_report.txt, in the order shown.  
           – No extra blank lines at the end of either file.  
           – The contents of both files exactly matching the criteria above.

    Only standard GNU/Linux text-processing utilities that are pre-installed in
    most distributions (e.g. mkdir, grep, wc, cat, head, printf, etc.) are
    required; do NOT assume any non-standard tools are available.

    NOTE: You do *not* have root privileges.  Work entirely inside /home/user.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing files that the grader will place inside the container
        before the agent starts.
    -->
    <preexisting>
        <file path="/home/user/support/logs/app.log"><![CDATA[[2023-07-01 10:00:01] INFO Starting service
[2023-07-01 10:01:15] ERROR Failed to connect to database
[2023-07-01 10:02:20] WARN Low disk space
[2023-07-01 10:03:25] INFO Service running
[2023-07-01 10:04:30] ERROR Timeout while reading response
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/support/logs/app2.log"><![CDATA[2023-07-01T11:00:00Z INFO scheduled job start
2023-07-01T11:00:05Z ERROR job crashed with code 1
2023-07-01T11:00:10Z INFO scheduled job end
2023-07-01T11:05:00Z ERROR unable to send email
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <!--
        Expected artefacts after successful task completion.
    -->
    <expected>
        <file path="/home/user/support/diagnostics/errors_combined.log"><![CDATA[[2023-07-01 10:01:15] ERROR Failed to connect to database
[2023-07-01 10:04:30] ERROR Timeout while reading response
2023-07-01T11:00:05Z ERROR job crashed with code 1
2023-07-01T11:05:00Z ERROR unable to send email
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/support/diagnostics/diagnostic_report.txt"><![CDATA[app.log total lines: 5
app2.log total lines: 4
total ERROR lines: 4
first ERROR line: [2023-07-01 10:01:15] ERROR Failed to connect to database
]]></file>
    </expected>
name: task_000000_cc5538c1
